boundary: Mappings certify only the stated formal kernel. Conditional and partial rows do not promote the full prose statement to a Lean theorem.

entries.note: Free energy and viability definitions.

theorems.name: Book5.viable_iff_positive_free_energy
theorems.statement: Viability is definitionally positive observer-resolved free energy.

entries.note: Requires the named second-law comparison and fixed-point calibration.

theorems.name: Book5.entropy_rate_nonnegative
theorems.statement: The declared second-law production comparison implies nonnegative entropy rate.

theorems.name: Book5.entropy_rate_eq_zero_iff_fixed
theorems.statement: Under explicit reflective-equilibrium calibration, zero entropy rate is equivalent to fixedness.

entries.note: The closed balance law is structure data, not a global Lean axiom.

theorems.name: Book5.energy_rate_eq_neg_entropic_rate
theorems.statement: A declared closed balance makes energy rate the negative temperature-weighted entropy rate.

entries.note: Persistence remains an explicit bridge law.

theorems.name: Book5.persists_iff_positive_throughout
theorems.statement: Under the named persistence law, persistence is equivalent to positivity throughout the interval.

entries.note: Proves the quotient threshold form only for positive minimum coupling.

theorems.name: Book5.covenant_stable_iff_threshold
theorems.statement: For positive minimum coupling, multiplicative covenant stability equals the quotient threshold form.

theorems.name: Book5.balanced_observer_weights_unique
theorems.statement: Present normalization and equal memory calibration uniquely select both weights as one.

theorems.name: closureMatrix_eigen_gold
theorems.statement: The balanced two-step closure has eigenvector (phi,1) with eigenvalue phi.

theorems.name: gold_unique_positive_root
theorems.statement: The golden ratio is the unique positive root of x^2=x+1.

theorems.name: balanced_memory_tendsto_gold
theorems.statement: Ratios of consecutive balanced Fibonacci memory amplitudes tend to phi.

entries.note: Integer two-support lower bound and elementary diagonal ratio.

atlas.name: $\sqrt{2}$ as the First Orthogonal Fracture Constant

theorems.name: sqrt2_first_fracture
theorems.statement: An integer transition with two nonzero coordinates has Euclidean length at least sqrt(2).

theorems.name: diag_fracture_ratio
theorems.statement: The elementary two-coordinate diagonal representability ratio is sqrt(2).

entries.note: Complete finite-support kernel: lower and cardinality upper bounds, finite p>1 and Linfinity equality characterizations, and the elementary diagonal values.

theorems.name: Book5.lpCostOn_le_axisCostOn
theorems.statement: The finite-support Lp cost is at most the L1 axis cost for p at least one.

theorems.name: Book5.axisCostOn_le_card_rpow_mul_lpCostOn
theorems.statement: Axis cost is at most card(s)^(1-1/p) times finite-support Lp cost.

theorems.name: Book5.sharp_norm_bound_iff_equal_magnitudes
theorems.statement: For finite p greater than one, the Book 5 norm bound is sharp iff all supported magnitudes agree.

theorems.name: Book5.infinity_ratio_bounds
theorems.statement: Every nonzero finite support has L1/Linfinity ratio between one and its cardinality.

theorems.name: Book5.infinity_sharp_iff_equal_magnitudes
theorems.statement: The Linfinity cardinality bound is sharp iff every supported magnitude equals the supremum.

theorems.name: Book5.diagonal_l1_l2_ratio
theorems.statement: Every nonzero equal-coordinate plane diagonal has l1/l2 ratio sqrt(2).

entries.note: Algebraic separation of phi and sqrt(2).

theorems.name: constants_complementary
theorems.statement: Phi and sqrt(2) satisfy distinct characteristic equations and are unequal.

entries.note: Arithmetic separation kernel only; the resonance-vs-fracture mechanism reading stays authored prose.

theorems.name: constants_complementary
theorems.statement: Phi and sqrt(2) satisfy distinct characteristic equations and are unequal.

entries.note: The norm and memory spectra are explicit independent product coordinates; sqrt(2) and phi coexist without identification.

theorems.statement: Euclidean fracture sqrt(2) and balanced memory phi coexist as distinct product coordinates.

theorems.name: Book5.elementary_spectrum_values
theorems.statement: The three elementary norm diagnostics have ratios 1, sqrt(2), and support size.

theorems.name: Book5.balancedSpectrum_memory
theorems.statement: Every balanced product spectrum has memory ratio phi.

theorems.name: Book5.norm_and_memory_coordinates_independent
theorems.statement: Norm and memory coordinates vary independently in the product spectrum.

entries.note: Transition-level diagnostic classification with independently varying norm and memory axes; not an essential identity of a manifold or agent.

theorems.name: Book5.norm_and_memory_coordinates_independent

theorems.name: Book5.changing_norm_preserves_balanced_memory
theorems.statement: Changing the norm diagnostic does not change balanced memory resonance.

entries.note: Typed product coordinates and their independence, not a universal manifold semantics.

atlas.name: Product Theorem of the Symbolic-Geometric Interface

theorems.name: Book5.same_memory_does_not_fix_norm
theorems.statement: A fixed balanced-memory classification does not determine the norm regime.

theorems.name: Book5.same_norm_does_not_fix_memory
theorems.statement: A fixed norm classification does not determine the memory regime.

theorems.name: closureMatrix_eigen_gold
theorems.statement: The balanced two-step closure has eigenvector (phi,1) with eigenvalue phi.

theorems.name: Book5.ObserverSpectralDecomposition.spectral_decomposition_kernel
theorems.statement: Reconstruction, coordinate uniqueness, and component invariance hold together.

entries.note: Weighted companion matrix and positive eigenpair.

theorems.name: Book5.reciprocityRate_characteristic
theorems.statement: The weighted reciprocity rate solves lambda^2=lambda+w for nonnegative weight.

theorems.name: Book5.reciprocityMatrix_eigen
theorems.statement: The weighted reciprocity matrix has eigenvector (lambda_w,1) at rate lambda_w.

entries.note: Spectral rate regimes only; ethical interpretation remains authored prose.

theorems.name: Book5.reciprocityRate_one
theorems.statement: Balanced reciprocity weight one has rate exactly phi.

theorems.name: Book5.reciprocityRate_subgolden
theorems.statement: Weights strictly between zero and one have rates strictly between one and phi.

theorems.name: Book5.reciprocityRate_supergolden
theorems.statement: Weights above one have rates strictly above phi.

entries.note: Covenant tuple as data with sign-carrying stability; the MAD dual is an involution that flips stability but not coupling strength.

theorems.name: Book5.dual_dual
theorems.statement: The MAD dual of a covenant is an involution.

theorems.name: Book5.dual_couplingStrength
theorems.statement: Coupling strength is invariant under polarity inversion while stability flips sign.

entries.note: The viability domain is definitionally the positive-free-energy region.

theorems.name: Book5.viable_iff_positive_free_energy
theorems.statement: Viability is definitionally positive observer-resolved free energy.

entries.note: MAP as positive-limit condition on the joint surplus sequence; membrane semantics not certified.

theorems.name: Book5.map_eventually_viable
theorems.statement: A joint surplus converging to a positive limit is eventually viable indefinitely.

entries.note: Convergence kernel only: positive limit forces joint indefinite viability; the coupling-threshold hypothesis enters via the contraction instance.

theorems.name: Book5.map_eventually_viable
theorems.statement: A joint surplus converging to a positive limit is eventually viable indefinitely.

theorems.name: Book5.map_joint_eventually_viable
theorems.statement: Two MAP surpluses share one horizon past which both stay positive.

entries.note: Scalar instance: contractive coupled ratio with positive carrying level yields MAP.

theorems.name: Book5.contraction_map
theorems.statement: A contractive coupled ratio with positive carrying level yields MAP from any start.

entries.note: Linear exchange model: the sign of stability decides viability vs collapse, and MAD is the exact polarity reflection of MAP.

theorems.name: Book5.covenant_map_viable
theorems.statement: Positive covenant stability makes the linear surplus eventually viable from any start.

theorems.name: Book5.covenant_mad_collapse
theorems.statement: Negative covenant stability collapses the linear surplus, proved by polarity reflection.

theorems.name: Book5.dual_surplus_reflect
theorems.statement: The dual covenant's linear surplus is the exact sign reflection of the original's.

theorems.name: Book5.viable_collapsed_exclusive
theorems.statement: Eventual viability and eventual collapse exclude each other.

entries.note: Assurance-game witness: cooperation and the trap are both Nash, cooperation payoff-dominant; general existence not proved.

theorems.name: Book5.cooperation_nash
theorems.statement: Mutual reflection is a MAP Nash point of the assurance game.

theorems.name: Book5.defection_nash
theorems.statement: The defection trap is also a Nash point: no unilateral step leaves it.

theorems.name: Book5.cooperation_dominates
theorems.statement: Cooperation strictly payoff-dominates the trap, so MAP must be chosen.

entries.note: Threshold-conversion kernel only; the phase-transition and fixed-point reading is not certified.

theorems.name: Book5.spectral_iff_thermal
theorems.statement: The spectral stability criterion holds exactly above the explicit critical temperature.

theorems.name: Book5.lambdaCrit_mono
theorems.statement: The stable region is monotone in symbolic temperature for positive densities and drifts.

entries.note: Typed transition diagnostics certify axis integrability, Euclidean fracture, and support collapse, including a concrete loss-of-distribution witness.

theorems.name: Book5.elementary_spectrum_values
theorems.statement: The three elementary norm diagnostics have ratios 1, sqrt(2), and support size.

theorems.name: Book5.support_collapse_forgets_distribution
theorems.statement: Linfinity can identify vectors with different coordinate distributions and L1 costs.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicTorsion_one
theorems.statement: The p=1 torsion phase is zero for every support size.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicTorsion_two_two
theorems.statement: The two-coordinate Euclidean torsion is sqrt(2)-1.

entries.note: Finite-support typed kernel assembling all norm regimes, equality cases, support collapse, diagonal decoherence, and the distinct memory resonance channel.

atlas.name: Fundamental Theorem of Norm-Induced Symbolic Fracture

theorems.name: Book5.fundamental_norm_fracture_kernel
theorems.statement: The elementary norm regimes, support-collapse witness, and unique distinct memory resonance assemble in one kernel theorem.

theorems.name: Book5.sharp_norm_bound_iff_equal_magnitudes
theorems.statement: For finite p greater than one, the Book 5 norm bound is sharp iff all supported magnitudes agree.

theorems.name: Book5.infinity_sharp_iff_equal_magnitudes
theorems.statement: The Linfinity cardinality bound is sharp iff every supported magnitude equals the supremum.

theorems.name: Book5.diagonalDecoherence_pos
theorems.statement: The diagonal representability gap is strictly positive for n>0.

entries.note: Exact finite-positive-p and Linfinity formulas, special values, monotonicity in positive finite p, and divergence of Linfinity torsion along unbounded support.

theorems.name: Book5.equalMagnitude_ratio
theorems.statement: A nonzero equal-magnitude vector attains the finite-support cardinality bound exactly.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicTorsion_eq_equalMagnitude_ratio_sub_one
theorems.statement: Symbolic torsion is the exact equal-magnitude norm ratio minus one.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicTorsion_one
theorems.statement: The p=1 torsion phase is zero for every support size.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicTorsion_two_two
theorems.statement: The two-coordinate Euclidean torsion is sqrt(2)-1.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicTorsion_mono_exponent
theorems.statement: For support size at least one, symbolic torsion is monotone in positive p.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicTorsionInfinity_eq_ratio_sub_one
theorems.statement: Infinity torsion n-1 is the exact equal-magnitude norm ratio minus one.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicTorsionInfinity_tendsto_atTop
theorems.statement: Infinity torsion diverges exactly along unbounded support cardinality.

entries.note: The logarithmic diagnostic is 2-1/p for finite p on support at least two, with the support-one convention and p=1,2,infinity values certified.

theorems.name: Book5.effectiveSupportDimension_eq
theorems.statement: For support size at least two, effective dimension is 2-1/p.

theorems.name: Book5.effectiveSupportDimension_one
theorems.statement: The p=1 effective support dimension is one.

theorems.name: Book5.effectiveSupportDimension_two
theorems.statement: The Euclidean effective support dimension is 3/2.

theorems.name: Book5.effectiveSupportDimensionInfinity_eq_two
theorems.statement: For support size at least two, infinity effective dimension is two.

theorems.name: Book5.effectiveSupportDimensionInfinity_log_ratio
theorems.statement: The logarithmic infinity ratio yields effective support dimension two.

entries.note: Finite admissible-path kernel with symbolic candidates explicitly included in geometric candidates; proves the exact minimum and representability criteria.

theorems.name: Book5.shortestGeometric_le_shortestSymbolic
theorems.statement: Minimizing over the geometric superset cannot cost more than minimizing over symbolic paths.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicDecoherence_pos_iff
theorems.statement: Decoherence is positive exactly when the geometric minimum is strictly shorter.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicDecoherence_eq_zero_iff_has_symbolic_geodesic
theorems.statement: Zero decoherence is equivalent to a symbolic path attaining the geometric minimum.

theorems.name: Book5.diagonal_symbolic_longer
theorems.statement: Every positive diagonal has shorter Euclidean than axis-generated length.

entries.note: For finite path families, decoherence is the nonnegative excess symbolic minimum, vanishing exactly when a symbolic geodesic exists; the diagonal gap is exact.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicDecoherence_nonneg
theorems.statement: Symbolic decoherence is a nonnegative excess cost.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicDecoherence_eq_zero_iff
theorems.statement: Decoherence vanishes exactly when symbolic and geometric minima agree.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolicDecoherence_eq_zero_iff_has_symbolic_geodesic
theorems.statement: Zero decoherence is equivalent to a symbolic path attaining the geometric minimum.

theorems.name: Book5.diagonalDecoherence_formula
theorems.statement: The n-by-n diagonal representability gap is n times 2-sqrt(2).

theorems.name: Book5.diagonalDecoherence_pos
theorems.statement: The diagonal representability gap is strictly positive for n>0.

entries.note: Phi is the unique positive balanced-recursion root in the memory coordinate and is proved distinct from the Euclidean norm ratio.

theorems.name: Book5.balanced_memory_resonance_unique
theorems.statement: Phi is the unique positive balanced two-step memory resonance root.

theorems.name: Book5.balancedSpectrum_memory
theorems.statement: Every balanced product spectrum has memory ratio phi.

theorems.name: Book5.euclidean_balanced_product_spectrum
theorems.statement: Euclidean fracture sqrt(2) and balanced memory phi coexist as distinct product coordinates.

entries.note: Balanced memory ratios converge to the unique phi coordinate, while sqrt(2) remains a distinct metric-fracture coordinate.

theorems.name: Book5.balanced_memory_converges_to_spectrum_memory
theorems.statement: Balanced Fibonacci memory ratios converge to the product spectrum memory coordinate.

theorems.name: Book5.balanced_memory_resonance_unique
theorems.statement: Phi is the unique positive balanced two-step memory resonance root.

theorems.name: Book5.euclidean_balanced_product_spectrum
theorems.statement: Euclidean fracture sqrt(2) and balanced memory phi coexist as distinct product coordinates.

entries.note: Given the stated observer-resolved linear equivalence and diagonal evolution law, four-channel reconstruction, uniqueness, and invariance are proved. Existence of such a decomposition is not asserted universally.

theorems.name: Book5.ObserverSpectralDecomposition.sum_components
theorems.statement: The four observer-resolved channel components reconstruct every transition.

theorems.name: Book5.ObserverSpectralDecomposition.coordinate_representation_unique
theorems.statement: The observer-resolved four-channel coordinate representation is unique.

theorems.name: Book5.ObserverSpectralDecomposition.component_is_invariant
theorems.statement: Every supplied spectral channel is invariant under the diagonal evolution.

theorems.name: Book5.ObserverSpectralDecomposition.spectral_decomposition_kernel
theorems.statement: Reconstruction, coordinate uniqueness, and component invariance hold together.

entries.note: Drift/reflection stabilization cancels only its budget channel. Zero energy derivative additionally requires external closure; a compiled countermodel refutes conservation from balance alone.

theorems.name: Book5.stabilized_energy_rate_eq_external
theorems.statement: Drift/reflection stabilization cancels only that channel, leaving the external energy rate.

theorems.name: Book5.closed_stabilized_energy_rate_zero
theorems.statement: A closed energy budget plus drift/reflection stabilization has zero energy derivative.

theorems.name: Book5.stabilized_conservation_iff_closed
theorems.statement: Under stabilization, zero energy derivative is equivalent to closure of the external channel.

theorems.name: Book5.balance_alone_does_not_force_conservation
theorems.statement: A concrete budget has balanced drift/reflection and a nonzero energy derivative.

entries.note: Represented as explicit MetabolicPersistenceLaw data, not a Lean axiom or a definitional implication.

theorems.name: Book5.metabolic_necessity
theorems.statement: Metabolic necessity follows only from an explicitly supplied MetabolicPersistenceLaw.

entries.note: Positive free-energy persistence characterizes life only under a supplied LifeLaw; agreement alone does not manufacture that bridge.

theorems.name: Book5.symbolic_life_criterion
theorems.statement: The life criterion follows only from an explicitly supplied LifeLaw.

theorems.name: Book5.agreement_on_positive_persistence_does_not_supply_life_law
theorems.statement: Cross-classifier agreement does not itself establish the life/persistence bridge.

entries.note: The corollary composes LifeLaw and MetabolicPersistenceLaw explicitly.

theorems.name: Book5.positive_persistence_has_metabolism
theorems.statement: Positive persistence yields metabolism only through both named bridge laws.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.energy_depletes_in_finite_time
theorems.statement: a strictly positive metabolic cost overrun drives the accumulated energy deficit negative in finite time

entries.note: the bound B=f(MC) with f nondecreasing is not modeled as a function; instead the geometric-growth consequence for recursion depth is proved directly from a budget hypothesis c0·k^n≤B.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.max_recursive_depth_bound
theorems.statement: geometric reflection-depth cost c0·k^n capped by budget B bounds depth n by a logb k (B/c0)

entries.note: n ≤ logb k (B/c0) is proved directly via Real.log monotonicity from the geometric-cost hypothesis.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.max_recursive_depth_bound
theorems.statement: geometric reflection-depth cost c0·k^n capped by budget B bounds depth n by a logb k (B/c0)

entries.note: the {F_proc<θ_proc} viability-set reading is mirrored by the admissible-complexity bound; the operator space Op(M) itself is not constructed.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.MetabolicBudget.complexity_le
theorems.statement: rearranging the cost law C·S ≤ α·MC gives the admissible-complexity bound C ≤ α·MC/S

entries.note: C, S, E_maint are represented as the structure's C, S, and the derived product form, packaged with the nonnegativity/positivity side-conditions as named fields (no axioms).

atlas.name: Operator Complexity, Stability Margin, Maintenance Cost

theorems.name: Book5Residue.MetabolicBudget.complexity_le
theorems.statement: rearranging the cost law C·S ≤ α·MC gives the admissible-complexity bound C ≤ α·MC/S

entries.note: C·S≤α·MC rearranges directly to C≤α·MC/S given S>0.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.MetabolicBudget.complexity_le
theorems.statement: rearranging the cost law C·S ≤ α·MC gives the admissible-complexity bound C ≤ α·MC/S

entries.note: the admissible-complexity bound α·MC/S is proved monotone nondecreasing in MC, for fixed α≥0,S>0.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.admissible_complexity_mono
theorems.statement: the admissible-complexity bound α·MC/S is monotone nondecreasing in metabolic capacity MC

entries.note: confirms the balanced closure matrix sits on the real-spectrum side of the discriminant trichotomy (consistent with φ being its real dominant eigenvalue, per Book5.closureMatrix_eigen_gold/gold_unique_positive_root); the subcritical/supercritical renormalization narrative is not modeled.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.closureMatrix_disc_pos
theorems.statement: Book5's balanced closure matrix (trace 1, det -1) has strictly positive discriminant 5

entries.note: the discriminant-based real/complex spectral split underlying MAD vs MAP/MAS is proved generically; the specific MAP-vs-MAS distinction (which real eigendirection is dominant) is not captured.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.quadratic_real_root_pos
theorems.statement: nonneg discriminant gives an explicit real root (T+√Δ)/2 of x²-Tx+D

theorems.name: Book5Residue.quadratic_real_root_neg
theorems.statement: nonneg discriminant gives an explicit real root (T-√Δ)/2 of x²-Tx+D

theorems.name: Book5Residue.quadratic_no_real_root_of_disc_neg
theorems.statement: negative discriminant forces x²-Tx+D to have no real root (the MAD/complex-spectrum case)

entries.note: proves the MAD (complex, disc<0) vs non-MAD (real, disc≥0) split and exhibits the closure matrix concretely on the real/MAP side; does not separate MAP from MAS within the real branch.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.quadratic_real_root_pos
theorems.statement: nonneg discriminant gives an explicit real root (T+√Δ)/2 of x²-Tx+D

theorems.name: Book5Residue.quadratic_no_real_root_of_disc_neg
theorems.statement: negative discriminant forces x²-Tx+D to have no real root (the MAD/complex-spectrum case)

theorems.name: Book5Residue.closureMatrix_disc_pos
theorems.statement: Book5's balanced closure matrix (trace 1, det -1) has strictly positive discriminant 5

entries.note: only the constant-rescaled-Fibonacci case of the ratio limit is proved; the anchor's general two-term holonomy/curvature recurrence (arbitrary balanced-closure initial data) is not derived.

atlas.name: Golden Ratio as Balanced Scale-Resonant Curvature Ratio

theorems.name: Book5Residue.balanced_memory_tendsto_gold_scaled
theorems.statement: any nonzero constant rescaling of the balanced two-step memory ratio still converges to φ

entries.note: F_bal is bounded below by F0 and attains F0 exactly at r=φ, for r,α>0.

atlas.name: Golden Ratio as Thermodynamic Optimum in the Balanced Regime

theorems.name: Book5Residue.golden_ratio_thermodynamic_min
theorems.statement: the balanced free-energy Lyapunov term F0+α(log r-log φ)² is bounded below by F0

theorems.name: Book5Residue.golden_ratio_thermodynamic_optimum_iff
theorems.statement: for r,α>0 the Lyapunov term attains its minimum F0 iff r=φ

entries.note: the irrational-scaling content of a diagonal transition is captured via the reused √2 compression ratio (and Book5.sqrt2_first_fracture); the decomposability characterization itself is not formalized.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.compression_ratio_R2
theorems.statement: the ℓ2 compression ratio 2/√2 = √2, re-exposing Book5's diagonal fracture ratio

entries.note: only the p=1 vs p=2 norm-domination case is proved concretely; the general ℓp class I_p for arbitrary p is not constructed.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.l1_ge_l2
theorems.statement: the ℓ1 norm of a finite real vector dominates its ℓ2 (Euclidean) norm

entries.note: κ_2(v)=‖v‖1/‖v‖2-1 ≥ 0 is proved for all finite real vectors.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.curvature_control_p2_nonneg
theorems.statement: the p=2 symbolic curvature control parameter ‖v‖1/‖v‖2 - 1 is nonnegative

theorems.name: Book5Residue.l1_ge_l2
theorems.statement: the ℓ1 norm of a finite real vector dominates its ℓ2 (Euclidean) norm

entries.note: all three predicted ratios R1=1, R2=√2, R∞=support-size are proved (R∞ via the equal-magnitude ℓ1 formula).

theorems.name: Book5Residue.compression_ratio_R1
theorems.statement: the ℓ1-to-ℓ1 compression ratio of any nonzero transition is 1

theorems.name: Book5Residue.compression_ratio_R2
theorems.statement: the ℓ2 compression ratio 2/√2 = √2, re-exposing Book5's diagonal fracture ratio

theorems.name: Book5Residue.l1_eq_card_mul_c
theorems.statement: for equal-magnitude-c transitions on a support s, the ℓ1 length equals c times the support size

entries.note: only the scalar factor κ_2(v) is proved nonnegative; the operator v ↦ κ_p(v)·v and its spectrum are not constructed.

theorems.name: Book5Residue.curvature_control_p2_nonneg
theorems.statement: the p=2 symbolic curvature control parameter ‖v‖1/‖v‖2 - 1 is nonnegative

entries.note: Stationary iff critical point of F_proc for the descent step; the minimizer/critical gap stays open.

theorems.name: Book5Op.operator_stationary_iff_critical
theorems.statement: The SRMF operator step is stationary iff at a critical point of the process free energy: the path rests iff F' = 0.

entries.note: SRMF convergence to a fixed point (contraction-Banach); the Wasserstein O(1/t) rate stays open.

theorems.name: Book5Op.contraction_flow_converges
theorems.statement: From any start the RG orbit converges to the unique fixed point: R^n(S) tends to S-star.

entries.note: The recursive reflective flow converges to a unique stable fixed point.

theorems.name: Book5Op.contraction_flow_unique_fixed_point
theorems.statement: A contraction on a complete space has a unique fixed point: the recursive-flow / RG map converges to one stable S-star.

entries.note: Operators evolve to and rest at the unique viable fixed point.

theorems.name: Book5Op.contraction_flow_unique_fixed_point
theorems.statement: A contraction on a complete space has a unique fixed point: the recursive-flow / RG map converges to one stable S-star.

entries.note: Metabolic capacity non-decreasing in energy reserves.

theorems.name: Book5Op.metabolic_capacity_monotone
theorems.statement: Metabolic capacity is non-decreasing in energy reserves.

entries.note: Viability antitone in spectral radius: minimizing rho maximizes viability.

theorems.name: Book5Op.viability_antitone_in_spectral_radius
theorems.statement: Viability is antitone in the coupling spectral radius: minimizing rho maximizes viability.

unmapped_claims.name: SRMF for Operator Selection and Evolution
Shade transfers, and the golden spiral paints equal shade-steps
